evals.prompt: Analyze Q1 budget vs actual. Revenue budget was $3M, actual was $2.85M. OpEx budget was $750K, actual was $840K.
evals.expected_output: 3-sheet variance report with flagging, commentary, and executive summary
expectations: Creates an .xlsx file with 3 sheets: Variance Summary, Executive Summary, Trend Analysis
Calculates OpEx variance as +12.0% (unfavorable for expense)
Flags OpEx as Critical (>10% over budget)
Generates automated commentary with possible drivers
Executive Summary highlights top variances and recommends actions

evals.prompt: Compare our March actuals to budget. All line items are within 3% of budget.
evals.expected_output: Clean variance report showing all items On Track with suggestion to lower thresholds
expectations: All line items flagged as On Track (green)
Suggests lowering variance thresholds for tighter monitoring
Still generates complete 3-sheet report for documentation

evals.prompt: Why is marketing over budget? Break down by subcategory.
evals.expected_output: Drill-down analysis of marketing subcategories with variance amounts and primary driver identification
expectations: Breaks marketing into subcategories (Digital Ads, Events, Agencies, etc.)
Shows variance amount and percentage for each subcategory
Identifies the primary driver of the overall marketing variance
Provides specific recommendations for each over-budget subcategory
